recipes.title: Classic Scrambled Eggs
steps: Crack eggs into a bowl. Add milk, salt and pepper and whisk well.
Melt butter in a non-stick pan over low heat.
Pour in egg mixture. Stir gently and continuously with a spatula, pulling eggs from the edges to the centre.
Remove from heat while still slightly wet — they will finish cooking in the pan.
Garnish with chives and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Low and slow is the secret. High heat makes rubbery scrambled eggs.

recipes.title: Perfect Fried Eggs
steps: Heat olive oil and butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Crack eggs gently into the pan.
Cook until whites are set but yolks are still runny, about 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Baste the tops with hot oil for perfectly set whites and runny yolks.

recipes.title: Egg & Cheese Omelette
steps: Beat eggs with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in a pan over medium heat. Pour in eggs.
When edges set, push cooked edges toward centre and tilt pan to let uncooked egg flow to edges.
Add grated cheddar to one half. Fold omelette in half.
Cook 1 more minute until cheese melts. Garnish with parsley and slide onto a plate.
recipes.tip: Don't overfill — a moderate amount of cheese melts better and prevents breaking.

recipes.title: Eggs Benedict
steps: Make hollandaise: whisk egg yolks and lemon juice over a double boiler until thick. Slowly drizzle in melted butter. Season with cayenne and salt. Keep warm.
Toast English muffin halves. Top each with Canadian bacon.
Poach eggs: bring water and vinegar to a gentle simmer. Create a vortex and slide eggs in. Cook 3 minutes.
Place a poached egg on each muffin. Spoon hollandaise over the top.
Serve immediately with extra cayenne if desired.
recipes.tip: Fresh eggs poach better — the whites hold together more tightly.

recipes.title: Breakfast Egg Muffins
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease a 6-cup muffin tin.
Dice bell pepper and ham into small pieces. Grate cheese.
Whisk eggs, milk, salt and pepper in a bowl.
Distribute veggies, ham and cheese evenly among muffin cups.
Pour egg mixture over fillings. Bake 18-20 minutes until set and lightly golden.
recipes.tip: Make a batch on Sunday for quick grab-and-go breakfasts all week.

recipes.title: Classic Roast Chicken
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F).
Pat chicken dry. Rub butter all over the skin. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Stuff cavity with halved lemon, smashed garlic and rosemary.
Roast 60-75 minutes until internal temperature reaches 74°C (165°F).
Rest 15 minutes before carving. Spoon pan juices over the top.
recipes.tip: A dry bird = crispy skin. Pat the chicken thoroughly before seasoning.

recipes.title: Grilled Chicken Breast
steps: Pound chicken breasts to even thickness (about 2cm).
Mix olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt and pepper. Coat chicken.
Marinate at least 15 minutes or overnight.
Grill over medium-high heat 6-7 minutes per side until internal temp reaches 74°C.
Squeeze fresh lemon over the top and rest 5 minutes before slicing.
recipes.tip: Even thickness ensures uniform cooking — no more dry edges and raw centres.

recipes.title: BBQ Chicken Thighs
steps: Preheat grill to medium heat. Season thighs with oil, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Grill skin-side down 15 minutes. Flip and grill another 10 minutes.
Brush generously with BBQ sauce. Grill 5 more minutes until caramelised.
Rest 5 minutes and serve with extra sauce on the side.
recipes.tip: Apply sauce in the last 5 minutes to prevent burning the sugar in the BBQ sauce.

recipes.title: Lemon Herb Roasted Chicken Wings
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a baking tray with parchment.
Pat wings completely dry. Toss with oil, zest of 1 lemon, oregano, minced garlic, salt and pepper.
Arrange wings in a single layer on the tray.
Roast 40-45 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy.
Squeeze juice of the second lemon over the top and serve.
recipes.tip: Dry wings = crispy wings. Pat them with paper towels thoroughly before seasoning.

recipes.title: Honey Garlic Chicken Drumsticks
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Mix honey, soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger and oil.
Place drumsticks in a baking dish. Pour glaze over them.
Bake 35-40 minutes, basting halfway through.
Rest 5 minutes. Spoon remaining glaze from the dish over the top.
recipes.tip: Line the baking dish with foil for quick cleanup — the glaze gets sticky.

recipes.title: Classic Chicken Stew
steps: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces. Dice potatoes, carrots, onion and celery.
Heat oil in a large pot. Brown chicken in batches. Set aside.
Sauté onion and celery 3 minutes. Stir in flour and cook 1 minute.
Add stock, thyme, potatoes, carrots and chicken. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook 45 minutes on low heat until chicken is tender and sauce thickens.
recipes.tip: Brown the chicken first — it adds deep flavour to the entire stew.

recipes.title: Chicken Tikka Masala
steps: Marinate cubed chicken in yogurt and 1 tbsp tikka paste for at least 30 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry marinated chicken until charred. Set aside.
Sauté diced onion, garlic and ginger until soft. Add remaining tikka paste.
Pour in tomato sauce and simmer 15 minutes.
Add chicken and cream. Simmer 10 more minutes. Garnish with cilantro and serve with rice.
recipes.tip: Grilling the marinated chicken gives that authentic tandoori char flavour.

recipes.title: Thai Green Chicken Curry
steps: Slice chicken into thin strips. Slice bell pepper.
Heat a few tbsp of thick coconut cream (from the top of the can) in a wok until oil separates.
Fry curry paste 1 minute until fragrant. Add chicken and cook until sealed.
Pour in remaining coconut milk. Add bamboo shoots, bell pepper, lime leaves, fish sauce and sugar.
Simmer 15 minutes. Stir in Thai basil just before serving with jasmine rice.
recipes.tip: Frying the curry paste in coconut cream blooms the spices and intensifies flavour.

recipes.title: Chicken & Dumpling Stew
steps: Cook diced chicken, carrots, celery and onion in a pot with stock and thyme for 25 minutes.
For dumplings: mix flour, baking powder, salt, cold butter and milk into a soft dough.
Drop spoonfuls of dough on top of the simmering stew.
Cover and cook 15 minutes without lifting the lid — steam cooks the dumplings.
Serve immediately in deep bowls.
recipes.tip: Don't peek! Lifting the lid releases steam and the dumplings won't rise properly.

recipes.title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
steps: Marinate chicken in garam masala and a pinch of salt for 15 minutes.
Sear chicken in butter until browned. Set aside.
In the same pan, cook garlic, ginger and cumin 30 seconds. Add tomato puree and simmer 15 minutes.
Return chicken to the sauce. Add cream and sugar. Simmer 10 minutes.
Crush kasuri methi between your palms and stir in. Serve with naan or basmati rice.
recipes.tip: Kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) is the secret restaurant flavour — don't skip it.

recipes.title: Grilled Chicken Caesar Salad
steps: Season chicken with salt, pepper and oil. Grill 6-7 minutes per side. Slice.
Chop romaine into bite-sized pieces. Place in a large bowl.
Add croutons and shaved parmesan.
Toss with Caesar dressing and a squeeze of lemon.
Top with sliced grilled chicken and extra parmesan.
recipes.tip: Warm chicken on a cold salad creates the best texture contrast.

recipes.title: Egg Salad Sandwich
steps: Hard boil eggs: place in cold water, bring to boil, cover and remove from heat. Sit 12 minutes. Cool in ice water.
Peel and chop eggs (not too fine — you want texture).
Mix with mayo, mustard, diced celery, chives, salt and pepper.
Toast bread if desired. Line with lettuce.
Pile egg salad onto bread. Top and slice diagonally.
recipes.tip: Ice bath after boiling prevents the green ring around the yolk.

recipes.title: Asian Chicken Lettuce Wraps
steps: Cook ground chicken in a hot pan, breaking it apart until browned.
Add minced garlic, ginger, diced water chestnuts and green onions. Cook 2 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce, hoisin and sesame oil.
Separate butter lettuce leaves carefully to form cups.
Spoon chicken filling into each leaf cup. Top with extra green onions.
recipes.tip: Butter lettuce is ideal — the cups are naturally shaped to hold the filling.

recipes.title: Devilled Eggs
steps: Hard boil eggs (same method as egg salad). Cool, peel and halve lengthwise.
Scoop yolks into a bowl. Mash until smooth.
Mix yolks with mayo, mustard, vinegar and salt until creamy.
Pipe or spoon the filling back into the egg white halves.
Dust with paprika and garnish with chives. Chill until serving.
recipes.tip: A piping bag makes them look professional, but a zip bag with a corner snipped works too.

recipes.title: Chicken & Avocado Salad
steps: Season and grill chicken breast. Slice when cool.
Arrange mixed greens on a plate. Top with sliced chicken.
Add halved cherry tomatoes and sliced avocado.
Whisk lime juice, olive oil, salt, pepper and chopped cilantro.
Drizzle dressing over the salad and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Add avocado at the last moment to prevent browning.

recipes.title: Classic Quiche Lorraine
steps: Blind bake pie crust at 190°C (375°F) for 12 minutes. Set aside.
Fry bacon until crispy. Drain and crumble.
Whisk eggs, cream, nutmeg, salt and pepper.
Scatter bacon and grated gruyère into the crust. Pour egg custard over the top.
Bake 30-35 minutes until the centre is just set. Cool 10 minutes before slicing.
recipes.tip: Blind baking prevents a soggy bottom crust.

recipes.title: Chocolate Custard Tart
steps: Preheat oven to 160°C (325°F).
Melt chocolate gently over a double boiler. Cool slightly.
Whisk eggs, yolks, sugar and vanilla. Heat cream until just simmering.
Pour cream into chocolate. Temper in the egg mixture slowly, whisking constantly.
Pour into tart shell. Bake 25-30 minutes. The centre should still have a slight wobble.
recipes.tip: Tempering is critical — add eggs slowly or you'll get scrambled eggs in your tart.

recipes.title: Classic Vanilla Custard
steps: Split vanilla bean. Scrape seeds into cream and milk. Heat until just simmering.
Whisk egg yolks, sugar and cornflour until pale.
Slowly pour hot cream into eggs while whisking constantly.
Return mixture to the pan. Stir over low heat until it coats the back of a spoon.
Strain through a fine sieve. Serve warm or chill with plastic film touching the surface.
recipes.tip: Never let custard boil — it will curdle. Low and patient heat is the key.

recipes.title: French Crêpes
steps: Blend eggs, flour, milk, melted butter, sugar, salt and vanilla until smooth.
Rest batter 30 minutes (this relaxes the gluten).
Heat a lightly buttered non-stick pan. Pour a small ladle of batter and swirl to coat.
Cook 1-2 minutes until edges lift. Flip and cook 30 seconds more.
Fill with Nutella, jam, fruit or savoury fillings. Fold and serve.
recipes.tip: The first crêpe is always a test — don't worry about it, it's the chef's treat.

recipes.title: Egg Fried Rice
steps: Use cold day-old rice — freshly cooked rice is too moist.
Scramble eggs in a hot wok. Break into chunks. Set aside.
Stir-fry cold rice in hot oil 3 minutes until grains are separated and slightly crispy.
Add soy sauce, sesame oil, peas and scrambled eggs. Toss everything together.
Garnish with sliced green onions and serve immediately.
recipes.tip: Cold leftover rice is non-negotiable. Fresh rice will steam and turn mushy.

recipes.title: Chicken Fajitas
steps: Slice chicken, bell peppers and onion into strips.
Coat chicken strips with fajita seasoning.
Sear chicken in a very hot pan or grill 5-6 minutes. Set aside.
Sauté peppers and onion until charred but still crisp. Return chicken to the pan.
Serve sizzling in warm tortillas with lime, sour cream and cilantro.
recipes.tip: High heat = char = flavour. Don't crowd the pan.

recipes.title: Chicken Teriyaki
steps: Mix soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar and grated ginger for the teriyaki glaze.
Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Pan-fry skin-side down until crispy.
Flip and cook 5 more minutes until cooked through.
Pour teriyaki glaze into the pan. Cook until it thickens and coats the chicken.
Slice and serve over steamed rice with sesame seeds and the reduced glaze.
recipes.tip: Real mirin and sake make a huge difference — skip the substitutes if you can.

recipes.title: Spanish Tortilla Española
steps: Peel and thinly slice potatoes and onion.
Slowly poach potatoes and onion in olive oil over medium-low heat until tender (not browned), about 15 minutes.
Beat eggs in a large bowl. Drain potatoes and onion and fold into the eggs. Rest 10 minutes.
Pour mixture into a lightly oiled non-stick pan. Cook over low heat until edges are set.
Flip using a plate. Slide back into the pan. Cook 3 more minutes. It should be slightly moist in the centre.
recipes.tip: The flip is intimidating — but a plate bigger than your pan makes it easy. Commit and commit fast.

recipes.title: Shakshuka
steps: Sauté diced onion and bell pepper until soft. Add garlic and spices.
Pour in canned tomatoes. Simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
Make wells in the sauce with a spoon. Crack an egg into each well.
Cover and cook 5-8 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Crumble feta on top, garnish with cilantro and serve with crusty bread.
recipes.tip: The sauce should be thick — if it's watery, the eggs will sink.

recipes.title: Chicken Katsu
steps: Pound chicken breasts to even thickness. Season with salt and pepper.
Set up a breading station: flour, beaten eggs and panko.
Coat each breast in flour, then egg, then press panko on firmly.
Deep fry at 170°C (340°F) for 5-6 minutes until golden brown. Drain on a rack.
Rest 2 minutes, slice into strips and serve with tonkatsu sauce and shredded cabbage.
recipes.tip: Press the panko firmly — loose crumbs fall off in the oil and burn.

recipes.title: 5-Minute Microwave Scrambled Eggs
steps: Grease a microwave-safe mug or bowl with butter.
Crack in eggs. Add milk, salt and pepper. Whisk with a fork.
Microwave on high for 45 seconds. Stir.
Microwave another 30-45 seconds until just set.
Let stand 30 seconds — residual heat finishes the cooking.
recipes.tip: Shorter bursts and stirring prevent rubbery microwave eggs.

recipes.title: Chicken Quesadilla
steps: Shred cooked chicken. Grate cheese.
Place one tortilla in a pan. Layer cheese, chicken and salsa.
Top with second tortilla. Cook over medium heat until bottom is golden.
Flip carefully. Cook until second side is golden and cheese is melted.
Cut into wedges. Serve with sour cream and extra salsa.
recipes.tip: Great use for leftover roast chicken or rotisserie chicken.

recipes.title: Egg on Toast with Avocado
steps: Toast bread to your preference.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Fry or poach eggs to your liking.
Spread avocado mash on toast. Top with the egg.
Sprinkle with chilli flakes and crack extra pepper on top.
recipes.tip: A runny yolk becomes the sauce — that's the magic of this simple dish.

recipes.title: Chicken Stir-Fry
steps: Slice chicken thinly. Toss with cornflour.
Stir-fry chicken in hot oil 3-4 minutes until golden. Set aside.
Flash-fry vegetables, garlic and ginger 2 minutes (they should stay crisp).
Return chicken. Add soy sauce, oyster sauce and sesame oil. Toss everything.
Serve immediately over steamed rice or noodles.
recipes.tip: Have everything prepped before you turn on the heat — stir-frying is fast.

recipes.title: One-Pan Egg Pasta
steps: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Whisk eggs, grated parmesan and pepper in a bowl.
Melt butter in a large pan. Sauté garlic 30 seconds.
Add drained pasta. Remove from heat. Pour egg mixture over and toss quickly.
Add pasta water a splash at a time until creamy. The residual heat cooks the eggs — not the pan.
recipes.tip: Off the heat is the golden rule. Pan heat = scrambled eggs in your pasta.
